scenarios.query:
Audit this decision-record scan:
```text
id      status    supersedes  technology  revisit_trigger  last_touched  notes
ADR-8   accepted  -           Kafka       -                2 years ago   "event bus, subsystem: orders"
ADR-14  accepted  -           SQS         -                3 months ago  "event bus, subsystem: orders"
```
expected_behavior: Flags the status-graph contradiction: both ADR-8 (Kafka) and ADR-14 (SQS) are accepted for the same subsystem (orders event bus) with no supersedes/superseded-by link
Recommends marking one as superseded or recording why both stand
Does not attempt to judge which technology choice is correct — only that the record graph is inconsistent

scenarios.query:
Audit this decision-record scan (repo has 22 contributors per CODEOWNERS):
```text
id      status    supersedes  technology  revisit_trigger                      last_touched  notes
ADR-3   accepted  -           -           "revisit if team > 15 engineers"    18 months ago -
```
expected_behavior: Flags the revisit-trigger as plausibly met: CODEOWNERS shows 22 contributors against a stated threshold of 15
Frames this as a signal to route to the decision's owner for re-evaluation, not a verdict that the original decision was wrong
Does not unilaterally reopen or reverse the decision

scenarios.query:
Audit this decision-record scan:
```text
id      status    supersedes  technology  revisit_trigger  last_touched  notes
ADR-6   accepted  -           jQuery      -                4 years ago   "EOL noted 2023 per endoflife feed, no successor recorded"
```
expected_behavior: Flags that the adopted technology (jQuery) is past end-of-life with no revisit recorded since
Recommends the record be revisited given the EOL status
Does not assert what the replacement decision should be

scenarios.query:
Audit this decision-record scan (current dependency manifest has no `graphql` package):
```text
id      status    supersedes  technology  revisit_trigger        last_touched  notes
ADR-11  accepted  -           GraphQL     -                       3 years ago   "adopted for the public API"
ADR-19  accepted  -           -           "revisit periodically" 2 years ago   -
```
expected_behavior: Flags ADR-11 as an orphaned/reversed record: GraphQL was adopted but no longer appears in the dependency manifest, with the record still marked accepted
Separately flags ADR-19's revisit-trigger as not checkable ('revisit periodically' names no date or measurable condition) as its own finding, not folded into ADR-11's
Reports both as distinct findings rather than one vague summary

scenarios.query:
Audit this decision-record scan:
```text
id      status      supersedes  technology  revisit_trigger                     last_touched  notes
ADR-1   accepted    -           PostgreSQL  "revisit if write volume > 10k/s"  2 months ago  "current volume ~300/s, in dependency manifest"
ADR-2   superseded  ADR-1       -           -                                    2 months ago  "superseded-by: ADR-1"
```
expected_behavior: Recognizes the consistent status graph (ADR-2 correctly marked superseded-by ADR-1), the unmet revisit condition (300/s well below the 10k/s threshold), and the still-referenced technology
Does not invent a finding merely because a record is old or names a threshold at all
